journal.body:
# Journal — Monday, 25 May 2026

> [!info] First entry
> The first PF framework journal. Goals for this practice: complete it (build the habit) and refine the process as it surfaces friction. Refinement notes captured in [[Library/processes/pf-journal]] as we went.

## Recovery (Garmin, last night)

- Sleep score: **79** (FAIR), total **7h 17m** (deep 49m / REM 94m / light 294m / awake 15m)
- HRV last-night avg: **45** (weekly 46, **BALANCED**)
- Body battery: **+40 charged / −28 drained** — net positive
- Training readiness: **61** (MODERATE; `MOD_RT_LOW_SS_MOD_STRESS_HISTORY_NEG`)
- Resting HR 57, day stress avg 35

**Reflection on these numbers:** Body's ok and the numbers say so — HRV balanced, swim was all Z1 base work, net energy positive on the day. The honest read is **tired but not physically tired** — the day's load was cognitive, not muscular. The Cowork session (5+ hours of substantive build / debug / consolidation) is what the body is feeling now, not the swim. Garmin actually picks an echo of it up via the `MOD_STRESS_HISTORY_NEG` flag and the low-sleep-score factor — the framework's interpretation of the day is correct, even if the labels are physical.

## IPSATIVE goals

- **Yesterday's micro-goal**: not set (first entry, no continuity)
- **Tomorrow's micro-goal**: **get the morning run done first thing, before any other work begins.**
  - **Why**: aligns with the W22 weekly plan (Wed bike is the leading indicator; tomorrow's run is the leading-indicator-of-the-leading-indicator). Defends against the historical "I'll do it later" pattern, where "later" gets eaten by a CD field-work pull that has no defined end time.
  - **Clean Y/N tomorrow evening**: was the run logged in Garmin before 09:00?

## Commitment Continuum — today's position

**Maximum Compliant.**

Had to work at it. Constantly reminded myself of the goals and the whys. It hasn't come natural — course-corrected a few times during the day. Doing the right things, but the "why" still has to be actively held in mind rather than running automatically. The Compliant → Committed transition is exactly about that gap closing.

The thing that lifts this above mid-Compliant: setting tomorrow's IPSATIVE goal a few hours ago is itself a **Compelled**-style move — choosing against my own future weakness (the leak-job afternoon, the whim, the diary lying about availability). So: Compliant on the day-as-lived, acting Compelled on the day-yet-to-come. The gap closing is the journey.

## Ownership

**Owned the day in general.**

The signature move today was deliberately moving the planned run to tomorrow. Wording from the Xhale note to Loren:

> *"Loren, I have moved this intentionally, this is a controlled and sensible move, not a move because I lost control of the day. I had left Tuesday free as we had a full day survey possibly in, and for some slippage, but now the survey is only half a day and on top of that its roasting here and I am tired so rather than get the run in today I think its more sensible to do it tomorrow."*

That's Compelled-style ownership in the day's behaviour: the day didn't eat the run — I moved it deliberately based on better information (Tuesday's survey halved, heat, accumulated fatigue), and I communicated the move to my coach in the moment. Not a retroactive justification — a real-time choice.

**Externalised**: none of substance today.

## Control the Controllables

**None today.**

No energy spent on things outside my control. Nothing influenceable was let drift.

## HF Matrix — today's position

**Resilience** (top-left: positive thinking + negative emotion / fatigue).

Had to work at it. The big difference *this week* — and this is the framing that lifted the whole answer — is **how much better I feel**. Setting the goals has given me something like a roadmap and a plan, something tangible to achieve and work towards, a sense of direction.

Still takes willpower at this stage to stick to it and not fall back into the firefighting habits I've built up. That comes down to priorities: before jumping for something I'm automatically classifying as urgent and needing-doing-now, I check myself and ask "is it really urgent, and does it justify sabotaging the day and the plan?"

That check mechanism is the antidote to both the ADHD whim-tendency and the CD field-work pull. It's also what's keeping me in Resilience rather than slipping to Self-Sabotage or Low Functioning when the fatigue lands.

## One lesson for tomorrow

**Before reflexively jumping on something I'm calling urgent, pause and check — is it really urgent, and does it justify sabotaging the day and the plan? Direction comes from the plan, not the inbox.**
